impact: The daemon was repeatedly generating invalid Python for the same validator. This repair keeps the work fixture-only, makes the next retry deterministic, removes the stale blocked marker from task selection, and adds explicit py_compile-first guardrails so autonomous progress can continue without broad contract rewrites or extractor implementation code.
summary: Repair PP&D supervisor guidance for the blocked checkbox-27 mutation-validator retry by replacing malformed pseudo-code guardrails with syntactically valid Python snippets and converting the blocked checklist item into a narrower syntax-first recovery task.
target_task: Supervisor repair: 1 task(s) are blocked: Task checkbox-27: Add a one-file mutation validator for `ppd/tests/fixtures/lifecycle/permit_lifecycle_requirement_extraction_expected_output.json` that first proves the committed `categoryRecords` fixture is valid, then mutates one field at a time for missing `categoryId`, missing `sourceEvidenceIds`, missing local `evidence[].evidenceId`, out-of-range `confidence`, duplicate `(categoryId, action, object, sourceEvidenceIds)` tuples, and malformed stop-gate classification. Do not add extractor implementation code.

command: files=$(find ppd -name '*.ts' -not -path '*/node_modules/*' -print); test -z "$files" || npx tsc --noEmit --target ES2020 --module ESNext --moduleResolution node --strict --skipLibCheck --types node $files
